title: Box Office Predictions: Alien Romulus to topple Deadpool & Wolverine?
text: This box office prediction piece examines whether Fede Alvarez's "Alien: Romulus" can dethrone the dominant "Deadpool & Wolverine" during its opening weekend. The author notes that while Disney has enjoyed tremendous success with crowdpleasers like "Inside Out 2" and "Deadpool & Wolverine", the "Alien" franchise has historically underperformed at the box office. He observes that "none have ever set the box office on fire outside the first two movies." The highest-grossing entry, "Prometheus", earned $126 million domestically. The critic predicts "Alien: Romulus" will open around $40 million, slightly below industry forecasts of $45-55 million. He anticipates the film will benefit from strong word-of-mouth and eventually dethrone the Marvel-Sony property, which he expects to earn approximately $35 million in its second week. "It Ends With Us" is predicted to capture third place with roughly $30 million. The full predicted rankings place "Romulus" first, followed by "Deadpool & Wolverine", "It Ends With Us", "Twisters", and "Despicable Me 4".
